q: Audible means the ability to be heard.
Audibility means the capability of detecting sounds in a certain range of frequencies.
The human ear is capable of detecting sounds of all possible frequencies.
The range of frequencies a person can hear is known as the range of audibility.
For humans with normal hearing, the lower limit of audibility is $20 Hz$.
The upper limit of audibility for humans is $200,000 Hz$.
The upper limit of audibility for humans is about $20,000 Hz$.
The limits of human hearing are known as the limit of audibility.
A frequency generator can be used to determine the audible frequency range.
Portable sound frequency generators can create sound from $0 Hz$ up to $22 kHz$.
Limit of audibility is the same for all mammals.
The audible range of a bat extends up to $150,000 Hz$.
Dolphins have an audible range from $10,000 Hz$ to $120,000 Hz$.
A cat can detect frequencies as high as $65,000 Hz$.
A dog's audible range is $15 Hz$ to $50,000 Hz$.
The audible range for an elephant is $20 Hz$ to $12,000 Hz$.
The sonar works based on the principle of echoes.
Sonar stands for sound navigation and ranging.
Ships use sonar to detect the position of other vessels.
Sonar can be used to find the depth of the sea.
A transducer in sonar is used only to receive signals.
A transducer in sonar is also known as a transmitter.
The interval for the reflected signal in sonar is called the echo.
Infrasound waves are waves with frequencies higher than $20 Hz$.
Infrasound waves have frequencies less than $20 Hz$.
Infrasound waves have long wavelengths.
Infrasound waves have short wavelengths.
Infrasound waves are able to travel over long distances.
Elephants communicate over distances up to $4 km$ using infrasound.
Elephants use fundamental frequencies as low as $15 Hz$.
A rhinoceros uses frequencies down to $10 Hz$ to communicate.
Infrasound waves travel longer distances with more attenuation.
Infrasound waves travel longer distances with less attenuation.
Ultrasound can be used in medical imaging.
Frequencies used in imaging are typically in the range of $1$ to $15 MHz$.
Humans can hear infrasound.
Humans can hear ultrasound.
A rhinoceros can communicate over distances further than $4 km$.
The hearing range of a human is broader than that of a dolphin.
The voicing range of a dog is $450 Hz$ to $1,080 Hz$.
The voicing range of a bat is $7,000 Hz$ to $120,000 Hz$.
The hearing range for a cat is $60 Hz$ to $65,000 Hz$.
The voicing range of an elephant is $5 Hz$ to $12,000 Hz$.
